title: eleocharis palustris subvar. calva
intro: Eleocharis palustris subvar. calva, also known as the naked variegated spike-rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family, Cyperaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eleocharis palustris subvar. calva include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Eleocharis palustris subvar. calva can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a treatment.
